title: System and method for gas mitigation
abstract: Increasing the pressure of a slurry by a filter press in which mineral oxide reacts with carbon dioxide enhances the formation of the carbonate by increasing the solubility and availability of carbon dioxide, accelerating reaction rates, shifting equilibria favorably, and promoting the formation of reaction intermediates like bicarbonate ions. A more efficient and faster synthesis of metal carbonates results.
claims: Claims We claim: 1. A system for gaseous mitigation, the system comprising: a slurry flow (1130) wherein the slurry flow includes water and one or more sequestered materials; a gas (1160) injected into the slurry flow (1130) forming a gasified slurry flow; a filter press (1110) configured to pressurize the gasified slurry flow thereby forming a mineralization environment; and a precipitate (1140) formed within the mineralization environment and in response to the gasified slurry flow being pressurized. 2. The system for gaseous mitigation according to claim 1, wherein the precipitate is a carbonate. 3. The system for gaseous mitigation according to claim 2, wherein the precipitate is one or more of Calcium Carbonate (CaCO3), Magnesium Carbonate (MgCO3), and/or Iron Carbonate (FeCO3). 4. The system for gaseous mitigation according to claim 2, wherein the precipitate is a crystalline, a polymorph or an amorphous. 5. The system for gaseous mitigation according to claim 1, wherein the gas is selected from the oxidizing group consisting of CO2, CH4, N2O, Halogens, PFC, Sf6 NF3, and HFC. 6. The system for gaseous mitigation according to claim 1, wherein the gas is selected from the reducing group consisting of Hydride, H-, K, Na, Ba, M, NaH, LiH, LiAIH, CaH2, and Ak. 7. The system for gaseous mitigation according to claim 1, further comprising a convergent / divergent apparatus (1 180, 400, 500) and wherein the convergent / divergent apparatus includes an inlet (610) whereby gas is inserted into the slurry flow. 8. The system for gaseous mitigation according to claim 1, wherein the one or more sequestered materials includes one or more oxide components selected from the group consisting of CaO, MgO, and FeO. 9. A process for gaseous mitigation, the process comprising: identifying (1010) a slurry flow wherein the slurry flow includes water and one or more sequestered materials; injecting (1020) a gas into the slurry flow forming a gasified slurry flow; pressurizing (1030), by a filter press, the gasified slurry flow thereby forming a mineralization environment; responsive to the gasified slurry flow being pressurized, forming(1040) a precipitate; dewatering (1050) the gasified slurry flow thereby stabilizing the precipitate. 10. The process for gaseous mitigation according to claim 9, wherein injecting includes controlling an amount of the gas injected thereby controlling alkalinity of the gasified slurry flow. 11. The process for gaseous mitigation according to claim 9, further comprising heating the gasified slurry flow thereby enhancing the mineralization environment. 12. The process for gaseous mitigation according to claim 9, further comprising directing the slurry flow through a convergent / divergent apparatus (1180, 400, 500) and wherein the convergent / divergent apparatus includes an inlet (610) whereby the gas is inserted into the slurry flow (405). 13. The process for gaseous mitigation according to claim 12, further comprising forming a cavitating turbulent flow in a mixing chamber downflow of the inlet, thereby combining the slurry flow with the gas. 14. The process for gaseous mitigation according to claim 9, further comprising inducing turbulent flow within the filter press. 15. The process for gaseous mitigation according to claim 9, further comprising controlling an amount of the gas injected thereby controlling acidity of the gasified slurry flow.
